key_insights.insight: Merely showing up is insufficient for success; it's the baseline expectation, not an achievement.
key_insights.quote: Showing up is none of the battle.

key_insights.insight: Obsessive focus and dedication are often misunderstood but are crucial for achieving exceptional results.
key_insights.quote: They can't see your level of obsession.

key_insights.insight: Winning often demands significant sacrifices, and it's essential to be aware of the potential costs.
key_insights.quote: Winning will cost you everything, but will reward you with so much more.

key_insights.insight: Decisions made in pursuit of success can have consequences, but failing to make tough choices leads to generational regret.
key_insights.quote: If you think the price of winning is too high, wait till you get the bill from regret.

key_insights.insight: Sacrifices made for success are often overlooked, while failures are magnified in people's perceptions.
key_insights.quote: Everybody remembers the one event you don't show up for.

key_insights.insight: Feedback and criticism are inherently the same; the difference lies in how they are perceived.
key_insights.quote: Now, people have a hard time understanding now the difference between feedback and criticism. It's exactly the same thing. It's just how you hear it.

key_insights.insight: You can learn from past experiences, but you can never truly 'unlearn' or 'unsee' them.
key_insights.quote: You can't unlearn what you've learned. You can't unlearn it.

actionable_advice.advice: Care deeply about the details and be willing to endure suffering to achieve the desired outcome.
actionable_advice.how_to: Invest time and effort into refining every aspect of your work, even if it requires extra effort or discomfort. Continuously seek improvement and refuse to settle for mediocrity.

actionable_advice.advice: Confront the potential 'bill of regret' by making hard decisions aligned with long-term goals.
actionable_advice.how_to: Evaluate the potential consequences of your decisions, both positive and negative. Weigh the short-term discomfort against the long-term benefits of achieving your goals and preventing regret.

memorable_stories: The story of having to urgently relocate the podcast set due to hotel issues, highlighting the commitment to details.
The anecdote about the guest's daughter asking if eating less would make him stay home more, showcasing the difficult choices involved in balancing career and family.
The guest flying from the West Coast to Chicago for a brief school performance of his daughter, demonstrating unseen sacrifices.
contrarian_claims: Challenging the common saying 'showing up is half the battle,' arguing that it's the bare minimum, not an achievement.
Suggesting that winning demands complete dedication and obsession, even at the expense of other areas of life (family, relationships), acknowledging that this approach has costs and consequences.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 114 - Winning Will Cost You More Than You Expect: Tim Grover
